get.description:
Returns all agents in your account, sorted by creation date with the most recent first. Use this endpoint to get an overview of your agents or to retrieve agent IDs for other API calls. Results are paginated with a default page size of 10.

Notes: Use the page query parameter to fetch additional results.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number to return

parameters.name: duration
parameters.description: The duration of the agents to list.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: The order of the agents to list. Defaults to desc.

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description: The orderBy to sort the results by given field

parameters.name: width
parameters.description: The width of the embed code. Defaults to 100%.

parameters.name: height
parameters.description: The height of the embed code. Defaults to 100%.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Filter the results by agent name

200.description: Successfully retrieved list of agents that belong to the user.

from.description: The first item number of the current page

per_page.description: The number of items per page

to.description: The last item number of the current page

post.description:
Creates a new agent by importing content from a sitemap URL or uploading a file. The imported content becomes the agent's knowledge base, which it uses to answer questions. The agent will begin processing the content immediately after creation.

Notes: Processing time depends on the amount of content. Check the agent's status to know when it's ready.

requestBody.description: Create a new agent from a sitemap or a file

is_ocr_enabled.description: OCR processing mode: 0 = Disabled, 1 = OCR enabled, 2 = AI Vision

is_vision_enabled.description: Whether to enable vision processing for images in the file source.

is_vision_compress_image.description: Whether to compress images for vision processing.

get.description: Returns the full configuration and current status for a specific agent. Use this to check processing status, view settings, or retrieve metadata about the agent.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The unique agent identifier.

parameters.name: width
parameters.description: The width of the embed code. Defaults to 100%.

parameters.name: height
parameters.description: The height of the embed code. Defaults to 100%.

post.description: Updates an existing agent's name or configuration settings. Use this to rename an agent or modify its basic properties without affecting its knowledge base.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the agent (formerly known as project).

is_shared.description: Whether the agent is public or not

is_ocr_enabled.description: OCR processing mode: 0 = Disabled, 1 = OCR enabled, 2 = AI Vision

delete.description:
Permanently deletes an agent and all associated data, including its knowledge base, conversations, settings, and analytics. This action cannot be undone.

Warning: This operation is irreversible. All conversations, sources, and settings will be permanently deleted.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the agent (formerly known as project).

post.description:
Creates a complete copy of an existing agent, including its knowledge base, persona, and settings. Use this to create variations of an agent for testing, or to use an existing agent as a template for a new one.

Notes: The cloned agent will have a new ID and can be modified independently of the original.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The unique agent identifier.

post.description:
Sends a message using the OpenAI-compatible request and response format. Use this if you are migrating from OpenAI or want to use existing OpenAI client libraries. Supports streaming for real-time responses.

Notes: This is a convenience endpoint for OpenAI compatibility. For full CustomGPT features, use the native message endpoint.

requestBody.description: Send a message in OpenAI chat completion format.

messages.description: The messages to send to OpenAI

model.description: ID of the model to use

labels.description: Optional source-label filter. List of label IDs (as strings) or names (project-scoped). Invalid values are ignored. Only the first 500 labels are processed.

labels_exclusive.description: If true, search only pages with provided labels. If false, search pages with provided labels plus unlabeled pages.

get.description: Returns usage metrics and performance statistics for an agent, including total conversations, query counts, document statistics, and processing information. Use this for monitoring and reporting.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the agent (formerly known as project).

get.description:
Returns all indexed documents in an agent's knowledge base. Each document represents a piece of content (webpage, PDF, uploaded file) that the agent can reference when answering questions. Results are paginated.

Notes: In the API, documents are called "pages" for backward compatibility.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The unique agent identifier.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number to return

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Limit the number of documents to return. Defaults to 20.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: The order of the documents to list. Sorts by page ID.

parameters.name: crawl_status
parameters.description: The crawl status of the documents to list.

parameters.name: index_status
parameters.description: The index status of the documents to list.

200.description: List of pages successfully retrieved.

from.description: The first item number of the current page

per_page.description: The number of items per page

to.description: The last item number of the current page

delete.description:
Permanently removes a document from an agent's knowledge base. The agent will no longer reference this content when answering questions. Use this to remove outdated or incorrect information.

Warning: This action cannot be undone. The document must be re-uploaded or re-indexed to restore it.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the agent (formerly known as project).

parameters.name: pageId
parameters.description: The page id

post.description:
Re-crawls and re-indexes a document to update its content in the knowledge base. Use this when the source content has changed and you want the agent to use the updated version.

Notes: Only works for URL-based documents. For uploaded files, delete and re-upload instead.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the agent (formerly known as project).

parameters.name: pageId
parameters.description: The page id

200.description: Updating Document with the new content

updated.description: Whether the process to update the Document started successfully or not

get.description: Returns metadata for a document, including its title, source URL, word count, and any custom metadata fields. Use this to display document information or manage your knowledge base.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The unique agent id.

parameters.name: pageId
parameters.description: The unique page id.

200.description: Get the document metadata data for a citation

put.description: Updates custom metadata fields for a document. Use this to add tags, categories, or other organizational information that helps manage your knowledge base.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The unique agent id.

parameters.name: pageId
parameters.description: The unique page id.

title.description: The title of the document used in citations

url.description: The url of the document used in citations

description.description: The description of the document used in citations

image.description: The url of the image used in citations

200.description: Get the document metadata data for a citation

get.description:
Returns all labels assigned to a document. Labels control which users can access answers derived from this document — users only see answers from documents matching their assigned labels.

Notes: Requires Custom plan. Documents without labels are accessible to all users.

put.description:
Assigns labels to a document for access control. The provided label IDs replace any existing labels on the document. To remove all labels (making the document accessible to everyone), send an empty array.

Notes: Maximum 10 labels per document.

Warning: This replaces all existing labels. Labels not included in the request will be removed from the document.

requestBody.description: Assign labels to a document, replacing any existing assignments

label_ids.description: List of label IDs to set for this page (replaces existing labels)

get.description: Returns the complete configuration for an agent, including persona instructions, response behavior, deployment options, and feature flags. Use this to review or back up an agent's configuration.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the agent (formerly known as project).

post.description:
Updates an agent's configuration, including persona instructions, response format, citation style, and deployment settings.

Notes: Only include the fields you want to change — omitted fields retain their current values.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the agent (formerly known as project).

chat_bot_avatar.description: This is the avatar that is shown in the agent response. You can make it a profile picture or your company logo.

chat_bot_bg.description: This is the background image shown in the agent conversations widget. You can change it to a company logo or background image.

chat_bot_bg_type.description: This is the background image shown in the agent conversations widget. You can change it to a company logo or background image.

chat_bot_bg_color.description: This is the background color shown in the agent conversations widget. You can change it to any color of your choice.

chat_bot_color_scheme.description: This is the color scheme of an agent.

radius_styling.description: This is the radius styling of an agent.

font_family.description: This is the font family of an agent.

default_prompt.description: This is the default prompt shown to the user. You can customize this for your company or client.

example_questions[].description: These are example questions shown to guide users. You can create customized questions to suit your company or client needs.

response_source.description: By default, we ask ChatGPT to use only your content in its response (recommended). If you wish ChatGPT to improvise and use its own knowledgebase as well, you can select "My Content + ChatGPT"

chatbot_msg_lang.description: By default, the agent messages like 'Ask Me Anything' are in English. You can customize this to your preferred language. Please note: This setting does not control what language ChatGPT responds in. That is controlled by the user's question. So a user asking in Portuguese, will most likely get a response from ChatGPT in Portuguese.

chatbot_color.description: Color of the agent in hex format

chatbot_toolbar_color.description: Color of the agent toolbar in hex format

persona_instructions.description: Customize your agent behavior by adjusting the system parameter to control its personality traits  and role.
persona_instructions.example: You are a custom AI Agent called CustomGPT.ai, a friendly lawyer who answers questions based on the given context.

citations_answer_source_label_msg.description: This is the message shown to indicate where the response came from. You can customize this message based on your business or language.
citations_answer_source_label_msg.example: Where did this answer come from?

citations_sources_label_msg.description: This is the message shown for the Sources label.  You can customize this message based on your business or language.

hang_in_there_msg.description: This is the message shown when the agent is thinking and waiting to answer. You can customize this message based on your tone, personality or language.

chatbot_siesta_msg.description: This is the message shown when the agent has encountered a problem or error. You can customize this message based on your tone, personality or language.
chatbot_siesta_msg.example: Oops! The agent is taking a siesta. We are aware of this and will get it back soon! Please try again later.

chatbot_failed_moderation_msg.description: This is the message shown when the agent fails moderation due to inappropriate content. You can customize this message based on your tone, personality or language.
chatbot_failed_moderation_msg.example: Sorry, I can't assist with that request. Please ask another question or let me know how I can help in a different way.

is_loading_indicator_enabled.description: Show animated loading indicator while waiting for a response from the agent

enable_citations.description: Each agent response shows an option for the user to see the sources/citations from your content from which the response was generated.

enable_feedbacks.description: Each agent response shows an thumbs up/down for the user to left own feedback.

enable_copy_button.description: Controls the visibility of the action button that lets users quickly copy the agent's entire response.

citations_view_type.description: Control how citations are shown. By default, the user can initiate to see the citations. You can choose to have it "Auto Shown" or "Auto Hide"

image_citation_display.description: Controls how images are displayed in citations. Default displays like regular citations, first_only inserts the image below the first paragraph where referenced.

limit_image_citation_height.description: Limit the height of image citations.

use_opengraph_image_citation.description: Use OpenGraph images from source pages when rendering image citations.

no_answer_message.description: This is the message shown when the agent cannot answer. You can customize it to a message asking the user to contact customer support or leave their email / phone.
no_answer_message.default: I'm sorry, I don't know the answer
no_answer_message.example: Sorry, I don't have an answer for that.

ending_message.description: You can instruct ChatGPT to end every response with some text like asking "Please email us for further support" (Not recommended for most use cases)
ending_message.example: Please email us for further support

try_asking_questions_msg.description: This is the message shown to the user to encourage them to ask questions. You can customize this message based on your tone, personality or language.

view_more_msg.description: This is the message shown to the user to view more starter questions. You can customize this message based on your tone, personality or language.

view_less_msg.description: This is the message shown to the user to view less starter questions. You can customize this message based on your tone, personality or language.

remove_branding.description: Controls what branding is shown at the bottom of the agent.

enable_recaptcha_for_public_chatbots.description: Should we check messages from guests with Recaptcha when your agent is publicly available (i.e. shared or embedded).

chatbot_model.description: This is the model used by the agent. You can choose a different model to suit your needs.

is_selling_enabled.description: Enable selling of the agent for monetization

hide_sources_from_responses.description: Should the agent mention source names?

input_field_addendum.description: You can add URL for your Terms of Service here, and it will be automatically added to your agent's interface.

chatbot_title_color.description: Chatbot title color, HEX color code

enable_inline_citations_api.description: Should the citations be shown inline in the response?

conversation_time_window.description: Set how long conversations remain active. [0] 24 hour memory limit - conversations stay open but agents only process messages from the last 24 hours. [1] Unlimited - conversations never expire and agents see the full history. [2] 24 hours, then close - conversations lock 24 hours after it started and users can't send new messages.

conversation_retention_period.description: Period after which we automatically delete all conversations.

conversation_retention_days.description: Custom period in days after which we automatically delete all conversations.

markdown_enabled.description: Enable Markdown support in the agent responses. This allows for rich text formatting in the agent responses, such as bold, italics, lists, and links.

is_hybrid_search.description: When enabled, we take a thorough look at any numeric codes in user's prompt (e.g. ABCD1234) to find the best match in the knowledge base. Use this if your agent is struggling with, for example, product names from uploaded spreadsheet. This feature might make agent's responses slower.

is_advanced_numeric_search.description: When enabled, advanced alphanumeric code extraction is applied at document indexing time via Pinecone metadata filtering for better numeric search results.

user_awareness.description: When enabled, the agent will be aware of the logged-in user's name and can use it in conversations.

get.description:
Returns the version history of an agent's persona. Every time the persona is updated, a snapshot is automatically saved, allowing you to view changes over time or restore a previous version. Results are paginated.

Notes: Requires Custom plan.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The unique agent identifier.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number to return

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Limit the number of documents to return. Defaults to 20.

200.description: List of previously saved agent personas.

from.description: The first item number of the current page

per_page.description: The number of items per page

to.description: The last item number of the current page

get.description:
Returns the full text and metadata for a specific persona version. Use this to compare versions or review what instructions were active at a particular time.

Notes: Requires Custom plan.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the agent (formerly known as project).

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: The version of the persona to set active.

200.description: Show a specific version of previously saved agent persona.

put.description:
Activates a previous persona version, making it the current active persona. This creates a new version entry in the history (it doesn't overwrite), preserving the full audit trail. Use this to roll back to a known-good configuration.

Notes: Requires Custom plan.

Warning: This doesn't delete newer versions — it creates a new entry based on the restored version.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the agent (formerly known as project).

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: The version of the persona to set active.

get.description:
Returns all labels configured for an agent, along with the users assigned to each label. Use this to review your access control configuration or build a label management interface.

Notes: Requires Custom plan.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The ID of the project

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number (pagination)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Items per page (pagination), max 100

200.description: List labels with assigned users for a project

from.description: The first item number of the current page

per_page.description: The number of items per page

to.description: The last item number of the current page

post.description:
Creates one or more new labels for document access control. Accepts an array of label names, allowing bulk creation. After creating labels, assign them to documents and users to control access.

Notes: Requires Custom plan.

requestBody.description: Create (or restore) multiple labels within a project by name

names.description: Label names to create/restore (max 20 characters each, must not start with a digit)

200.description: Create (or restore) labels within a project

put.description:
Sets which users are assigned to each label. The provided user list becomes the source of truth — users not included in the list are removed from that label, and new users are added.

Notes: Requires Custom plan.

Warning: This is a replacement operation, not an append. Users not included in the request will be removed from the label.

labels.description: List of labels with users to assign

id.description: Label ID (must belong to this project)

users.description: List of user emails to assign to this label

200.description: Update labels and assigned users for a project

from.description: The first item number of the current page

per_page.description: The number of items per page

to.description: The last item number of the current page

get.description: Retrieves a specific label, including its name and the list of users assigned to it.

200.description: Get a single label with assigned users for a project

users.description: List of user emails assigned to this label for the project

put.description: Updates the name of an existing label. User and document assignments are preserved.

requestBody.description: Rename a label within a project

name.description: New label name (must not start with a digit)

200.description: Rename a label within a project

delete.description:
Permanently deletes a label and removes all associated assignments (both user-to-label
and document-to-label mappings). Documents that lose their only label will become
accessible to everyone.

200.description: Delete label-user mappings for a project

deleted.description: Indicates whether the delete operation was successful

delete.description:
Removes specific users from one or more labels without affecting other users'
assignments. Use this when offboarding users or revoking access to specific content.

labels.description: List of labels and users to remove

users.description: List of user emails to remove from this label

200.description: Delete label-user mappings for a project

deleted.description: Indicates whether the delete operation was successful

get.description: Retrieve the plugin details associated with an agent (formerly known as project). This endpoint provides information about the agent plugin.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The unique agent id.

post.description: Create a new plugin for a specific agent (formerly known as project) identified by its unique projectId. This endpoint enables you to create a custom plugin for the project. Which allows you to share the custom agent as openai plugin to be shared globally.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The id of an agent (formerly known as project) in which plugin will be stored.

description.example: Trusted information about indoor plants and gardening.

is_active.description: Whether the agent plugin is active or not

put.description: Update the plugin configuration for a specific agent (formerly known as project) identified by its unique projectId. This endpoint allows you to update the associated attributes of the plugin.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The id of an agent (formerly known as project) in which plugin will be stored.

description.example: Trusted information about indoor plants and gardening.

is_active.description: Whether the agent plugin is active or not

get.description: Returns all conversations for an agent. Each conversation represents a chat session with a user and contains the full message history. Results are paginated and sorted by most recent activity.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The ID of the agent to get the conversations for

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number to return

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: The order to sort the results in given direction

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description: The orderBy to sort the results by given field

parameters.name: userFilter
parameters.description: Filter the results by the user that created the conversation

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Filter the results by conversation name

parameters.name: lastUpdatedAfter
parameters.description: Only conversations that received message(s) after this date will be returned in results. If not set, the parameter is ignored.

200.description: List of conversations for an agent

from.description: The first item number of the current page

per_page.description: The number of items per page

to.description: The last item number of the current page

post.description:
Creates a new conversation session for an agent. Returns a session ID that you'll use to send messages. You can optionally provide a name to help identify the conversation later.

Notes: You can also create conversations implicitly by sending a message with a new session ID.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the agent (formerly known as project).

get.description: Exports the complete conversation history in a downloadable format. Use this to archive conversations, share transcripts, or analyze conversation content offline.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The ID of the agent to get the messages for

parameters.name: sessionId
parameters.description: The session ID of the converstaions to get the messages for

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: File format

parameters.name: isCustomRowsNumber
parameters.description: Export only given rows number

parameters.name: rowsNumber
parameters.description: Custom rows number

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Start date for filtering data

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: End date for filtering data

put.description: Updates conversation metadata, such as the display name. Use this to rename conversations for easier organization and identification.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the agent (formerly known as project).

parameters.name: sessionId
parameters.description: The unique session identifier.

name.description: The new name of the conversation

200.description: Update conversation information

delete.description:
Permanently deletes a conversation and all its messages. Use this to remove sensitive conversations or clean up test data.

Warning: This action cannot be undone. All messages in the conversation will be permanently deleted.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the agent (formerly known as project).

parameters.name: sessionId
parameters.description: The unique session identifier.

get.description: Returns all messages in a conversation, including user prompts, agent responses, timestamps, and citations. Use this to display conversation history or analyze past interactions.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The ID of the agent to get the messages for

parameters.name: sessionId
parameters.description: The session ID of the converstaions to get the messages for

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number to return

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: The order to sort the results by

parameters.name: includeInsights
parameters.description: When true, includes customer_intelligence data in the response.

post.description:
Sends a user message to an agent and returns the agent's response. Supports streaming for real-time display, file attachments for document analysis, and persona overrides for dynamic behavior. This is the primary endpoint for chat interactions.

Notes: Add ?stream=true to receive the response in real-time chunks.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The ID of the agent to get the messages for

parameters.name: sessionId
parameters.description: The session ID of the converstaions to get the messages for

parameters.name: stream
parameters.description: Whether to stream the response or not, if stream the responses will be sent as data-only server-sent events as they become available, with the stream terminated by a `status: "finish"` message. [Example Python code](https://github.com/Poll-The-People/customgpt-cookbook/blob/main/examples/SDK_Create_a_new_conversation_and_send_a_message_to_the_conversation.ipynb).

parameters.name: lang
parameters.description: The language to use for the prompt

parameters.name: external_id
parameters.description: The external ID of the prompt history.

requestBody.description: Send a message to a conversation.

prompt.example: Write me hello world program in C

custom_persona.description: Custom persona to use for the conversation
custom_persona.example: You are a custom AI Agent called *agent name*, a friendly *agent role* who works for *organization* and answers questions based on the given context. Be as helpful as possible. Always prioritize the customer. Escalate complex issues. Stay on topic. Use appropriate language, Acknowledge limitations.

chatbot_model.description: Agent model to use for the conversation

response_source.description: By default, we ask ChatGPT to use only your content in its response (recommended). If you wish ChatGPT to improvise and use its own knowledgebase as well, you can set this to "openai_content".

custom_context.description: Custom context to use for the conversation
custom_context.example: User details: Name: John Doe, Role: Software Developer, Organization: CustomGPT.ai

file.description: File to upload (Allowed types: pdf, docx, doc, odt, txt, jpg, jpeg, png, webp)

files[].description: Multiple files to upload (Allowed types: pdf, docx, doc, odt, txt, jpg, jpeg, png, webp)

agent_capability.description: Agent capability to use for the conversation.

labels[].description: Optional source-label filter. List of label IDs or names (project-scoped). Invalid values are ignored. Only the first 500 labels are processed.

labels_exclusive.description: If true, search only pages with provided labels. If false, search pages with provided labels plus unlabeled pages.

get.description: Returns the complete details for a single message, including the user's prompt, the agent's response, timestamps, citations, and any attached metadata. Use this to retrieve specific messages for display or analysis.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The ID of the agent to get the messages for

parameters.name: sessionId
parameters.description: The session ID of the conversation to get the messages for

parameters.name: promptId
parameters.description: The message id of the message

parameters.name: includeInsights
parameters.description: When true, includes customer_intelligence data in the response.

put.description: Records user feedback (thumbs up/down) for a message. Use this to collect quality signals that help identify which responses are helpful and which need improvement.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The ID of the agent to get the messages for

parameters.name: sessionId
parameters.description: The session ID of the conversation to get the messages for

parameters.name: promptId
parameters.description: The message id of the message

parameters.name: includeInsights
parameters.description: When true, includes customer_intelligence data in the response.

get.description: Returns the factual claims extracted from an agent's response. Each claim is a discrete statement that can be independently verified against the source documents. Use this for fact-checking or building trust indicators.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The ID of the agent to get the messages for

parameters.name: sessionId
parameters.description: The session ID of the conversation to get the messages for

parameters.name: promptId
parameters.description: The message id of the message

get.description: Returns a verification trust score for a message, calculated by checking how well the agent's claims are supported by the source documents. Higher scores indicate better-grounded responses.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The ID of the agent to get the messages for

parameters.name: sessionId
parameters.description: The session ID of the conversation to get the messages for

parameters.name: promptId
parameters.description: The message id of the message

post.description: Triggers a fact-checking verification process for a message's claims. The system compares each claim against the source documents and reports which claims are supported, partially supported, or unsupported.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The ID of the agent to get the messages for

parameters.name: sessionId
parameters.description: The session ID of the conversation to get the messages for

parameters.name: promptId
parameters.description: The message id of the message

get.description: Returns metadata for a specific citation, including the source document's title, description, URL, and preview information. Use this to display source attribution or allow users to verify the agent's sources.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The unique project identifier.

parameters.name: citationId
parameters.description: The unique citation identifier.

200.description: Get the document metadata data for a citation

get.description: Returns all data sources connected to an agent. Each source represents an origin for documents — such as a sitemap, Google Drive folder, SharePoint site, or uploaded files. Use this to manage what content feeds into an agent's knowledge base.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the agent (formerly known as project).

post.description:
Connects a new data source to an agent's knowledge base. You can add content via sitemap URL, file upload, or integration connection (Google Drive, SharePoint, etc.). The system will begin indexing the content immediately.

Notes: For large sources, indexing may take several minutes. Check the source status to monitor progress.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the agent (formerly known as project).

requestBody.description: Create a new agent source from a sitemap or a file

is_ocr_enabled.description: OCR processing mode: 0 = Disabled, 1 = OCR enabled, 2 = AI Vision

is_vision_enabled.description: Whether to enable vision processing for images in the file source.

is_vision_compress_image.description: Whether to compress images for vision processing.

image_extraction_type.description: The method used for extracting images from the website.

put.description: Updates configuration for a data source, such as auto-sync frequency, crawl depth, or file filters. Use this to fine-tune how the source is indexed and kept up to date.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the agent (formerly known as project).

executive_js.description: Whether the agent source should execute JavaScript

data_refresh_frequency.description: The agent source data refresh frequency

create_new_pages.description: Add new pages to agent automatically during refresh agent source

remove_unexist_pages.description: Remove pages from agent automatically during refresh agent source

refresh_existing_pages.description: Refresh existing page during refresh agent source

refresh_schedule.description: Custom schedule for advanced data frequency

days.description: Index of days in which sitemap should be refreshed. Starts from 0 (Sunday) to 6 (Saturday).

hours.description: List of times in which sitemap should be refreshed. Must be in HH:MM format.

allow_extra_timeout.description: Allow extra timeout for agent source

image_extraction_type.description: The method used for extracting images from the website.

update_existing_images.description: Whether to update existing images during refresh.

delete.description:
Removes a data source and all its documents from an agent's knowledge base. Use this to disconnect content that's no longer relevant or to clean up after testing.

Notes: To delete individual documents without removing the entire source, use the Delete Document endpoint instead.

Warning: This deletes ALL documents from this source. The action cannot be undone.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the agent (formerly known as project).

parameters.name: sourceId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of a source.

200.description: Agent source deleted successfully

deleted.description: Whether the agent source was deleted successfully or not

put.description:
Triggers an immediate re-sync of a data source, refreshing all its documents with the latest content. Use this when you know the source has been updated and don't want to wait for the scheduled sync.

Notes: Only available for sources that support syncing (sitemaps, integrations). Not available for file uploads.

parameters.name: sourceId
parameters.description: The source id

get.description: Returns AI-analyzed insights about your users, including common intents, emotional sentiment, frequently discussed topics, and emerging trends. Use this to understand what users are asking about and how they're feeling.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The ID of the project to get customer intelligence for

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number for pagination

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of items per page

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: Start date for filtering data

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: End date for filtering data

parameters.name: content_source[]
parameters.description: Filter results by Content Source (use repeated parameters, e.g. content_source[]=value)

parameters.name: accuracy
parameters.description: Filter results by Verified Claims Score as range (format: min-max, e.g., 0-100)

parameters.name: stakeholder_status[]
parameters.description: Filter results by Stakeholder Status (use repeated parameters, e.g. stakeholder_status[]=value)

parameters.name: user_emotion[]
parameters.description: Filter results by User Emotion (use repeated parameters, e.g. user_emotion[]=value)

parameters.name: user_intent[]
parameters.description: Filter results by User Intent (use repeated parameters, e.g. user_intent[]=value)

parameters.name: language[]
parameters.description: Filter results by Query Language (use repeated parameters, e.g. language[]=value)

parameters.name: user_id[]
parameters.description: Filter results by Query Sent By (use repeated parameters, e.g. user_id[]=value)

parameters.name: external_id[]
parameters.description: Filter results by CRM ID (use repeated parameters, e.g. external_id[]=value)

parameters.name: country[]
parameters.description: Filter results by User Location (use repeated parameters, e.g. country[]=value)

parameters.name: risk_fidelity[]
parameters.description: Filter results by Risk: Fidelity (use repeated parameters, e.g. risk_fidelity[]=value)

parameters.name: risk_jailbreak[]
parameters.description: Filter results by Risk: Jailbreak (use repeated parameters, e.g. risk_jailbreak[]=value)

parameters.name: risk_prompt_leakage[]
parameters.description: Filter results by Risk: Prompt Leakage (use repeated parameters, e.g. risk_prompt_leakage[]=value)

parameters.name: risk_profanity[]
parameters.description: Filter results by Risk: Profanity (use repeated parameters, e.g. risk_profanity[]=value)

parameters.name: request_source[]
parameters.description: Filter results by Deployment (use repeated parameters, e.g. request_source[]=value)

parameters.name: leads[]
parameters.description: Filter results by Lead Captured (use repeated parameters, e.g. leads[]=value)

get.description: Returns user traffic metrics, including unique visitors, session counts, geographic distribution, and device types. Use this to understand who's using your agent and how they're accessing it.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The ID of the agent to get traffic for

parameters.name: filters
parameters.description: Filters to return traffic metrics for.

200.description: Get the traffic metrics for an agent

get.description: Returns aggregate query metrics, including total query volume, success rates, average response times, and failure reasons. Use this to monitor agent performance and identify issues.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The ID of the agent to get queries for

parameters.name: filters
parameters.description: Filters to return queries metrics for.

200.description: Get the query metrics for an agent

get.description: Returns conversation metrics, including total conversations, average duration, messages per conversation, and completion rates. Use this to understand how users engage with your agent.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The ID of the agent to get conversations for

parameters.name: filters
parameters.description: Filters to return conversations metrics for.

200.description: Get the conversation metrics data for an agent

get.description: Returns time-series data formatted for charts, with daily, weekly, or monthly breakdowns of key metrics.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The ID of the agent to get analysis for

parameters.name: filters
parameters.description: Filters to return graph-ready analysis data for.

parameters.name: interval
parameters.description: Interval to return graph-ready analysis data inbetween.

200.description: Get the graph-ready data analysis for an agent

get.description: Exports lead information captured from conversations, including email addresses, names, and any custom fields collected. Use this to sync leads with your CRM or marketing tools.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The ID of the project to get leads export for

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: Start date for the export range (ISO 8601 format)

parameters.description: End date for the export range (ISO 8601 format)

parameters.description: Number of items per page

get.description: Returns all access licenses configured for an agent. Licenses control who can interact with the agent and what permissions they have. Use this to manage access distribution.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the agent (formerly known as project).

post.description: Creates a new access license for an agent with specified permissions and optional expiration. Use this to grant controlled access to external users or systems.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the agent (formerly known as project).

get.description: Returns the full details for a specific license, including permissions, usage statistics, status, and expiration date. Use this to audit or troubleshoot access issues.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the agent (formerly known as project).

parameters.name: licenseId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of a license.

put.description: Updates a license's settings, such as permissions, expiration date, or active status. Use this to modify access without creating a new license.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the agent (formerly known as project).

parameters.name: licenseId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of a license.

name.description: The new name of the license

delete.description:
Revokes and permanently removes an access license. Any users or systems using this license will immediately lose access to the agent.

Warning: This immediately revokes access for anyone using this license.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the agent (formerly known as project).

parameters.name: licenseId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of a license.

get.description: Returns a preview of the source document referenced by a citation. Use this to show users the original content that the agent used to generate a response.
Returns profile information for the currently authenticated user, including account details and settings.

200.description: Retrieve user account info

post.description: Updates profile settings for the currently authenticated user. Use this to modify account preferences or personal information.

200.description: Update user account info

get.description:
Searches for team members by email address or user ID. Use this to find users when assigning permissions or managing team access.

Notes: Requires Owner or Admin role.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email of the team member to search for

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: User ID of the team member to search for

200.description: Team member found successfully

get.description: Returns your current usage and plan limits for agents, storage (words), and monthly queries. Use this to monitor consumption, plan for scaling, or display usage dashboards to your users.

200.description: Retrieve user usage limits

max_projects_num.description: The maximum number of agents allowed for this team.

current_projects_num.description: The total number of agents currently belonging to this team.

max_total_storage_credits.description: The maximum number of storage credits allowed for this team.

current_total_storage_credits.description: The amount of storage credits currently in use.

max_queries.description: The maximum number of queries allowed per billing cycle for this team.

current_queries.description: The number of queries used in the current billing cycle.

id.description: The unique identifier of the document

description.example: This domain is for use in illustrative examples in documents. You may use this domain in literature without prior coordination or asking for permission.

page_url_hash.description: Hash of the URL of the document

s3_path.description: This is the path where the document is stored in S3.\nNote: This is omitted in the response if the document is not a file

index_status.description: Is the document available to agent

is_file.description: Whether the document is a file or not

is_refreshable.description: Whether the document can be refreshed or not

is_file_kept.description: Whether the file is kept after processing or not.\nNote: This is omitted in the response if the document is not a file

filename.description: Filename of the document.\nNote: This is omitted in the response if the document is not a file

filesize.description: Filesize of the document.\nNote: This is omitted in the response if the document is not a file

created_at.description: Date and time when the document was created

updated_at.description: Date and time when the document was updated

deleted_at.description: Date and time when the document was deleted

created_at.description: Date and time when the persona was created

average_queries_per_conversation.description: Average number of queries per conversations

total.description: Total number of queries over all conversations

query_status.description: Number of successful and failed queries over all conversations

ProjectLabelUsers.description: Label with assigned users for a specific project

users.description: List of user emails assigned to this label for the project

project_id.description: Agent ID this license belongs to

created_at.description: Date and time when the license was created

updated_at.description: Date and time when the license was last updated

description.example: Trusted information about indoor plants and gardening.

is_active.description: Whether the agent plugin is active or not

is_chat_active.description: Whether the agent is active or not

user_id.description: User ID of the agent owner

team_id.description: Team ID of the agent owner

created_at.description: Date and time when the agent was created

updated_at.description: Date and time when the agent was last updated

deleted_at.description: Date and time when the agent was deleted

is_shared.description: Is the agent public or not

shareable_slug.description: Shareable slug that can be used to share the agent

shareable_link.description: Shareable link that can be used to share the agent

embed_code.description: Embed code that can be used to embed the agent

live_chat_code.description: Live chat code that can be used to embed the live chat

are_licenses_allowed.description: Whether licenses are allowed or not

subdomain_slug.description: Subdomain slug that can be used to share the agent

chatbot_avatar.description: This is the avatar that is shown in the agent response. You can make it a profile picture or your company logo.

chatbot_background_type.description: Here you can choose whether the agent conversations widget will use background image or background color.

radius_styling.description: Here you can choose radius styling.

font_family.description: Here you can choose font family.

chatbot_background.description: This is the background image shown in the agent conversations widget. You can change it to a company logo or background image.

chatbot_background_color.description: This is the background color of the agent conversations widget. You can set it to any valid color hex.

chatbot_color_scheme.description: Here you can choose color scheme of an agent.

default_prompt.description: This is the default prompt shown to the user. You can customize this for your company or client.

example_questions.description: These are example questions shown to guide users. You can create customized questions to suit your company or client needs.

response_source.description: By default, we ask ChatGPT to use only your content in its response (recommended). If you wish ChatGPT to improvise and use its own knowledgebase as well, you can select "My Content + ChatGPT"

chatbot_msg_lang.description: By default, the agent messages like 'Ask Me Anything' are in English. You can customize this to your preferred language. Please note: This setting does not control what language ChatGPT responds in. That is controlled by the user's question. So a user asking in Portuguese, will most likely get a response from ChatGPT in Portuguese.

chatbot_color.description: Color of the agent in hex format

chatbot_toolbar_color.description: Color of the agent toolbar in hex format

persona_instructions.description: Customize your agent behavior by adjusting the system parameter to control its personality traits  and role.
persona_instructions.example: You are a custom agent assistant called CustomGPT.ai, a friendly lawyer who answers questions based on the given context.

citations_answer_source_label_msg.description: This is the message shown to indicate where the response came from. You can customize this message based on your business or language.
citations_answer_source_label_msg.example: Where did this answer come from?

citations_sources_label_msg.description: This is the message shown for the Sources label.  You can customize this message based on your business or language.

hang_in_there_msg.description: This is the message shown when the agent is thinking and waiting to answer. You can customize this message based on your tone, personality or language.

chatbot_siesta_msg.description: This is the message shown when the agent has encountered a problem or error. You can customize this message based on your tone, personality or language.
chatbot_siesta_msg.example: Oops! The agent is taking a siesta. We are aware of this and will get it back soon! Please try again later.

chatbot_failed_moderation_msg.description: This is the message shown when the agent fails moderation due to inappropriate content. You can customize this message based on your tone, personality or language.
chatbot_failed_moderation_msg.example: Sorry, I can't assist with that request. Please ask another question or let me know how I can help in a different way.

is_loading_indicator_enabled.description: Show animated loading indicator while waiting for a response from the agent

enable_citations.description: Each agent response shows an option for the user to see the sources/citations from your content from which the response was generated.

enable_feedbacks.description: Each agent response shows an thumbs up/down for the user to left own feedback.

enable_copy_button.description: Controls the visibility of the action button that lets users quickly copy the agent's entire response.

citations_view_type.description: Control how citations are shown. By default, the user can initiate to see the citations. You can choose to have it "Auto Shown" or "Auto Hide"

image_citation_display.description: Controls how images are displayed in citations. Default displays like regular citations, first_only inserts the image below the first paragraph where referenced.

limit_image_citation_height.description: Limit the height of image citations.

use_opengraph_image_citation.description: Use OpenGraph images from source pages when rendering image citations.

no_answer_message.description: This is the message shown when the agent cannot answer. You can customize it to a message asking the user to contact customer support or leave their email / phone.
no_answer_message.default: I'm sorry, I don't know the answer
no_answer_message.example: Sorry, I don't have an answer for that.

ending_message.description: You can instruct ChatGPT to end every response with some text like asking "Please email us for further support" (Not recommended for most use cases)
ending_message.example: Please email us for further support

try_asking_questions_msg.description: This is the message shown to the user to encourage them to ask questions. You can customize this message based on your tone, personality or language.

view_more_msg.description: This is the message shown to the user to view more starter questions. You can customize this message based on your tone, personality or language.

view_less_msg.description: This is the message shown to the user to view less starter questions. You can customize this message based on your tone, personality or language.

remove_branding.description: Controls what branding is shown at the bottom of the agent.

private_deployment.description: When Private Agent Deployment is enabled you can add agent’s  code snippet to any website, but users will be able to access it only if they login to their CustomGPT.ai (or IdP) account

enable_recaptcha_for_public_chatbots.description: Should we check messages from guests with Recaptcha when your agent is publicly available (i.e. shared or embedded).

chatbot_model.description: This is the model used by the agent. You can choose a different model to suit your needs.

is_selling_enabled.description: Enable selling of agent for monetization

hide_sources_from_responses.description: Should the agent mention source names?

input_field_addendum.description: You can add URL for your Terms of Service here, and it will be automatically added to your agent's interface.

chatbot_title_color.description: Chatbot title color, HEX color code

enable_inline_citations_api.description: Should the citations be shown inline in the response?

conversation_time_window.description: Set how long conversations remain active. [0] 24 hour memory limit - conversations stay open but agents only process messages from the last 24 hours. [1] Unlimited - conversations never expire and agents see the full history. [2] 24 hours, then close - conversations lock 24 hours after it started and users can't send new messages.

conversation_retention_period.description: Period after which we automatically delete all conversations.

conversation_retention_days.description: Custom period in days after which we automatically delete all conversations.

markdown_enabled.description: Enable Markdown support in the agent responses. This allows for rich text formatting in the agent responses, such as bold, italics, lists, and links.

is_hybrid_search.description: When enabled, we take a thorough look at any numeric codes in user's prompt (e.g. ABCD1234) to find the best match in the knowledge base. Use this if your agent is struggling with, for example, product names from uploaded spreadsheet. This feature might make agent's responses slower.

is_advanced_numeric_search.description: When enabled, advanced alphanumeric code extraction is applied at document indexing time via Pinecone metadata filtering for better numeric search results.

user_awareness.description: When enabled, the agent will be aware of the logged-in user's name and can use it in conversations.

agent_capability.description: Agent capability preset to use for the project.

executive_js.description: Whether the agent source should execute JavaScript

data_refresh_frequency.description: The agent source data refresh frequency

create_new_pages.description: Add new pages to agent automatically during refresh agent source

remove_unexist_pages.description: Remove pages from agent automatically during refresh agent source

refresh_existing_pages.description: Refresh existing page during refresh agent source

image_extraction_type.description: The method used for extracting images from the website.

update_existing_images.description: Whether to update existing images during refresh.

id.description: The unique identifier of the prompt history.

conversation_id.description: The unique identifier of the conversation.

user_id.description: The unique identifier of the user.

citations.description: The citations for the prompt history.

user_ip.description: The IP address of the user.

user_agent.description: The user agent of the user.

external_id.description: The external ID of the prompt history.
